title: FreshStop at Astron Energy George Central turns 1 - See what the buzz is about!
description: BUSINESS NEWS - There's more than just...
bodyText:
Advertorial
                            Gallery

BUSINESS NEWS - There's more than just fuel at FreshStop at Astron Energy George Central, and if you haven't stopped by lately, now's the time to discover what's happening at this local gem on Courtenay Street, opposite Outeniqua High School.


> On Wednesday 30 July, the station marked one year as Astron Energy with a festive birthday bash, thanking customers with in-store specials, supplier giveaways and free party packs for kids.


Two lucky shoppers even won back what they spent - and one grand prize draw is still coming for those who visited on the day.

They are also proud to be the 323rd site nationally to convert from Caltex, marking an exciting new chapter.

But this store has been serving the community long before the rebrand. The FreshStop and Seattle Coffee have been local favourites for years, and in April, added Crispy Chicken - some say it's George's most affordable tasty treat.


> PHOTO GALLERY - Astron Energy George Central celebrates their first birthday


Owners Patrick and Noelle Ellis, along with their passionate team - Sandley, Tracey, Marja, Thembisa, Lorenzo and Warren - are building more than a business. "We're creating a space where people feel welcome, and we're deeply invested in our community."

That commitment includes support for Eden FM Soup Drives, the Night Shelter George, the Rotary Club George, Africa Skills, Carpe Diem School and the Kwagga Fund - with upcoming drives planned for Women's Day and Christmas.

Curious? Stop by for coffee, crispy snacks or fresh deals - and see why this stop is more than just a service station.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
